archetype: High-Precision Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) Interstitial Fluid Lag Compensation & Hypoglycemia Risk Forecasting Engine
domain: Class II/III Medical Device Software & Diabetes Management
description: A real-time embedded inference system that processes raw electrochemical sensor data to predict blood glucose levels 30-60 minutes ahead, compensating for physiological lag and sensor noise. It is used by Type 1 Diabetics and clinicians to automatically adjust insulin pump delivery rates or trigger urgent alerts, requiring sub-second latency and absolute algorithmic determinism to prevent life-threatening under-dosing or overdosing.
rules: MUST implement algorithms compliant with IEC 62304 for Medical Device Software Lifecycle processes, ensuring traceability from requirements to code.
MUST process data streams adhering to IEEE 11073 Patient Data Application Profile (PDAP) for real-time glucose transmission between devices.
MUST guarantee a maximum latency of <200ms from sensor read to clinical decision output to meet safety-critical timing constraints.
MUST utilize deterministic floating-point arithmetic or fixed-point libraries to ensure bitwise reproducibility across different hardware revisions.
MUST enforce strict cryptographic signing of all firmware updates and data logs to satisfy FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use non-deterministic garbage collection pauses in the main control loop, as this can cause missed insulin delivery windows leading to DKA.
DO NOT rely on cloud-based latency for real-time hypoglycemia alerts; the critical safety logic must reside on the edge device due to network unreliability.
DO NOT use probabilistic AI models without a fallback deterministic rule set, as unexplained 'black box' decisions are unacceptable for FDA clearance.
key_technical_challenges: Calibrating the mathematical model to account for the 10-15 minute physiological lag between blood glucose and interstitial fluid glucose concentrations under varying stress levels.
Filtering high-frequency electrochemical noise from the sensor without introducing phase lag that would invalidate the predictive window.
Managing thermal drift and battery voltage fluctuations in the sensor signal processing chain to maintain accuracy over a 14-day sensor lifespan.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees without a garbage collector, essential for preventing runtime panics in safety-critical loops.
recommended_stack.framework: Zephyr RTOS — A lightweight, real-time operating system with built-in support for medical device certification and low-power peripherals.
recommended_stack.database_engine: SQLite (WAL mode) or Flatbuffers — Ensures atomic, crash-resistant logging of sensor data and treatment events for post-mortem forensic analysis.
